items.q: What do you call a baby kangaroo?
items.explanation: A joey develops first in its mother's womb and then continues growing in the pouch after birth.

items.q: A square has five sides.
items.explanation: A square is a four-sided polygon with all sides equal and all interior angles at 90 degrees.

items.q: What piece of safety gear protects a worker's head on a construction site?
items.explanation: Hard hats are designed to reduce injury from falling objects and other head impacts on building sites.

items.q: What card game uses terms such as hit, stand, and bust?
items.explanation: Blackjack players try to reach 21 without going over, while beating the dealer's hand.

items.q: Which author created Winnie-the-Pooh?
items.explanation: Milne based the Pooh stories partly on the stuffed animals belonging to his son Christopher Robin.

items.q: What animal is famous for changing color to help it blend with surroundings?
items.explanation: Chameleons can shift skin color for camouflage, temperature regulation, and communication.

items.q: Sharks are fish.
items.explanation: Sharks are fish with skeletons made of cartilage rather than bone, but they still belong to the fish group.

items.q: What heavy object is dropped from a ship to hold it in place?
items.explanation: An anchor grips the seabed so a vessel can remain in one place instead of drifting with wind or current.

items.q: What tool with rungs is used for climbing up to a roof or high shelf?
items.explanation: A ladder provides spaced rungs or steps so a person can climb safely to a higher level.

items.q: What device measures short intervals of time with sand flowing between two glass bulbs?
items.explanation: An hourglass tracks time as sand falls at a steady rate from one bulb to the other.

items.q: Which color model do most computer screens use to display images?
items.explanation: Screens emit light, so they build color additively with red, green, and blue rather than mixing inks on paper.

items.q: A kilometer is longer than a mile.
items.explanation: A mile is about {{measure|1.60934|km|2}}, so a kilometer is shorter than a mile.

items.q: What was the capital of the Aztec Empire on the site of present-day Mexico City?
items.explanation: Tenochtitlan stood on an island in Lake Texcoco and was joined to the mainland by causeways before the Spanish conquest.

items.q: Which strait separates Sicily from mainland Italy?
items.explanation: The Strait of Messina is a narrow channel between northeastern Sicily and Calabria at the toe of Italy's mainland.

items.q: What legal order requires a person to appear in court or produce documents?
items.explanation: A subpoena can compel testimony or documents, and ignoring one can lead to penalties for contempt of court.

items.q: Which painter created The Birth of Venus?
items.explanation: Botticelli's painting of Venus emerging from the sea is one of the most famous works of the Italian Renaissance.

items.q: Saturn is the only planet with rings.
items.explanation: Jupiter, Uranus, and Neptune also have ring systems, although Saturn's are the most prominent.

items.q: What instrument measures the angle between a celestial object and the horizon for navigation?
items.explanation: Navigators use a sextant with time data to help determine position at sea by measuring celestial angles.

items.q: Which engineer designed the internal framework of the Statue of Liberty?
items.explanation: Before his tower made him famous worldwide, Eiffel contributed the iron support structure inside the Statue of Liberty.

items.q: Which gulf lies between the Baja California Peninsula and mainland Mexico?
items.explanation: The Gulf of California, also called the Sea of Cortez, separates the Baja California Peninsula from the Mexican mainland.

items.q: What branch of linguistics studies meaning in words and sentences?
items.explanation: Semantics examines meaning, while syntax focuses on structure and pragmatics considers meaning in context and use.

items.q: The empty set is a subset of every set.
items.explanation: In set theory, there is no element in the empty set that could violate the condition for being a subset.

items.q: What type of galaxy is the Milky Way commonly classified as?
items.explanation: Astronomers classify the Milky Way as a spiral galaxy with a central bar-shaped structure crossing its bulge.

items.q: What grammatical case commonly marks the indirect object in Latin?
items.explanation: The dative case in Latin often marks the recipient or beneficiary of an action rather than the direct object.

items.q: What philosophical school was founded by Zeno of Citium?
items.explanation: Stoicism began in Athens and later shaped Roman thinkers such as Seneca, Epictetus, and Marcus Aurelius.

items.q: Which political work by Niccolo Machiavelli offers advice to rulers on power and statecraft?
items.explanation: Written in the early sixteenth century, The Prince became one of the most debated works of political theory in Europe.

items.q: Every continuous function is differentiable.
items.explanation: A function can be continuous everywhere yet fail to have a derivative at some points, as with the absolute value function at zero.

items.q: What legal term describes a law that punishes an act retroactively?
items.explanation: Ex post facto laws are generally prohibited in many legal systems because they punish conduct that was not illegal when it occurred.

items.q: What theological term refers to God becoming human in Jesus Christ?
items.explanation: In Christian theology, the Incarnation refers to the Word taking on human nature in the person of Jesus.

items.q: What manuscript discovery near Qumran included ancient Jewish texts on scrolls?
items.explanation: The Dead Sea Scrolls were found in caves near Qumran and include biblical manuscripts and other Second Temple texts.
